_description: 8 augmentations purchasable with Augmentation Points. Cost = number of augmentations already owned + 1 (including current). Max 1 per campaign turn. Applies to all current and future characters (except Bots and Soulless imports).
_notes: Augmentations take effect starting from the following campaign turn after purchase.

augmentations.name: Boosted Decision Making
augmentations.description: Psionic engineering improves preternatural senses. Each battle round, you may pick up and reroll one of your Initiative dice.

augmentations.name: Boosted Recovery
augmentations.description: Implantation of carefully selected plant DNA accelerates recovery from injury and illness. When a character is sent to Sick Bay, reduce the initial recovery time by 1 turn. No effect on Grunts.

augmentations.name: Claws
augmentations.description: Retractable claws are convenient for labor tasks as well as enhancing defensive capabilities. All characters count as having a Damage +0 Melee weapon which cannot be lost.

augmentations.name: Enhanced Mobility
augmentations.description: Adaptation of animal DNA to improve short-term running speed. Characters moving in a straight line may increase their base move by 1". Dash moves are now 3" instead of 2".

augmentations.name: Enhanced Vision
augmentations.description: Improves conventional vision and latent psionic senses. All visibility limits are increased by +3".

augmentations.name: Inherent Protection
augmentations.description: Adaptive skin that can harden in response to minor injuries. All characters receive a 6+ Saving Throw against incoming damage. Normal rules for combined Saving Throws apply.

augmentations.name: Mental Links
augmentations.description: Connecting individuals together for rapid consensus-based decision making. Counts as a Milestone for campaign completion. Also adds 1 Story Point.

augmentations.name: Psionic Cohesion
augmentations.description: Improves psionic connections to increase cohesion and morale. Any character that would lose a level of Loyalty rolls 1D6. On 5-6, they do not lose a level.
